_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

I've now done a thorough review of the entire codebase. Here are my findings:

---

## Dead Code & Cleanup Opportunities

### 1. `DependencyModal` is completely unused (dead code)

`src/components/dashboard/DependencyModal.tsx` is never imported or rendered by any other component. The only file that references `DependencyModal` is itself. This entire file (154 lines) can be deleted.

### 2. Alert system is fully wired but never triggered (dead infrastructure)

The entire alert infrastructure exists but `addAlert` is never called outside its own context definition:
- `src/context/AlertContext.tsx` — defines `AlertProvider`, `useAlerts`, `addAlert`
- `src/components/layout/AlertContainer.tsx` — renders alerts
- `src/components/ui/Alert.tsx` — the alert UI component

`AlertContainer` is rendered in `AppLayout`, and `AlertProvider` wraps the app in `main.tsx`, but nothing in the app ever calls `addAlert`. This means:
- **3 files / ~80 lines** are effectively dead code
- The `AlertItem` type in `src/types/index.ts` is only used by this dead system
- The `AlertProvider` wrapper in `main.tsx` is doing nothing

If you plan to use alerts eventually, this is fine to keep — but if it's leftover from iteration, it's all removable.

### 3. `src/assets/react.svg` is unused

This is the default Vite scaffold asset. Nothing imports it.

### 4. `DATA_CATEGORY_COLORS` in `colors.ts` is likely dead weight

The `DATA_CATEGORY_COLORS` map (lines 68-99) maps specific full-path category strings to colors. However, when `fidesMode` is on, the app uses `FIDES_GROUP_COLORS` instead, and when it's off the filter chips still pass individual category strings to `getColorForValue` — but the color lookup only has 5 specific entries so most categories will fall through to `DEFAULT_COLOR`. This map is technically reachable but seems like a remnant from before the Fides Group feature was added. It has significant overlap with `FIDES_GROUP_COLORS` (Device = cyan, Contact = green, Location = yellow, Financial = pink). Consider whether you actually need both — if all category display now goes through Fides Groups, `DATA_CATEGORY_COLORS` can go.

### 5. `resolveSystem` in `DependencyModal` and `SystemDetail` is redundant

Both `DependencyModal.tsx` (line 34) and `SystemDetail.tsx` (line 18) do this pattern:

```typescript
const resolved = resolveSystem(system.fides_key) ?? system;
```

These components already receive a `System` object as a prop, so resolving it again from the lookup map is unnecessary — it will always return the same object (or fall back to the same prop). This is a no-op that adds confusion. Both files could simply use the `system` prop directly and drop the `resolveSystem` import.

### 6. `DependencyModal`'s `resolveSystem` import becomes doubly dead

Since `DependencyModal` is itself dead code (#1), its `resolveSystem` import is dead too. But even if the modal were used, per #5, the resolve would be redundant.

### 7. Duplicate Fides Group category map computation

`getFidesGroupCategoryMap` is called in two places:
- `FilterBar.tsx` (line 61-67) — to show secondary labels under filter chips
- `SwimlaneBoard.tsx` (line 44-47) — to show secondary text under swimlane headers

Both compute the same map from the same `systems` array. This could be computed once higher up (in `DashboardPage`) and passed down to both, avoiding the duplicate work.

### 8. `DATA_CATEGORY_LABELS` and `DATA_USE_LABELS` overlap with display logic

In `formatLabel.ts`, there are hardcoded lookup maps for 5 category labels and 5 data use labels. The `classifyCategory` function in `categoryClassification.ts` also has its own `FIDES_GROUP_MAP` with 15 entries. If the Fides Group approach is now the primary display mode, the granular `DATA_CATEGORY_LABELS` in `formatLabel.ts` may be unnecessary — though they're still used when fidesMode is off, so this is more of a "keep an eye on" item.

---

## Summary of actionable cleanups, ordered by impact

| Priority | Item | Lines saved | Files removable |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------------|
| High | Delete `DependencyModal.tsx` (unused) | ~154 | 1 |
| High | Delete alert system (`AlertContext`, `AlertContainer`, `Alert`) if not planned | ~80 | 3 |
| Medium | Remove `AlertItem` from types, `AlertProvider` from `main.tsx` | ~10 | — |
| Medium | Remove redundant `resolveSystem` calls in `SystemDetail` | ~3 | — |
| Low | Delete `src/assets/react.svg` | — | 1 |
| Low | Lift `getFidesGroupCategoryMap` to avoid duplicate computation | — | — |
| Low | Evaluate removing `DATA_CATEGORY_COLORS` from `colors.ts` | ~30 | — |

The biggest wins are deleting `DependencyModal.tsx` and — if you don't plan to use alerts — removing the entire alert system. Those alone would remove about 230 lines of dead code across 4 files. Switch to Agent mode if you'd like me to make any of these changes.

---

_**User**_

Please make these changes but keep the alerrt system - I'm going to covert the checkboxes into a little settings area which wil show an alert as a 'Toast' when the user successfully updates

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

All clean — zero TypeScript errors and no lint issues. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Deleted (dead code):**
- `src/components/dashboard/DependencyModal.tsx` — entirely unused component (154 lines)
- `src/assets/react.svg` — default Vite scaffold asset, never imported

**Cleaned up `SystemDetail.tsx`:**
- Removed the redundant `resolveSystem` call and import — the component already receives the `System` object as a prop, so re-resolving it was a no-op

**De-duplicated `getFidesGroupCategoryMap`:**
- Both `FilterBar` and `SwimlaneBoard` were independently computing the same Fides group map from the same data. Lifted the computation into `DashboardPage` and passed the result down as a `fidesGroupMap` prop to both components, removing the duplicate `useMemo` calls and the `getFidesGroupCategoryMap` / `useMemo` imports from each child.

**Kept intact (as requested):**
- The entire alert system (`AlertContext`, `AlertContainer`, `Alert`, `AlertItem` type) — ready for your toast notifications when the settings area is built.
